REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO ZBS LAW LLP FOR ALL CAUSES ACTION, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING DEFECTS IN THE DEED AND ZBS LAW LLP'S MALICE. THE DISMISSAL OF BONYM'S PRIOR LAWSUIT DID NOT CONSTITUTE A JUDICIAL DETERMINATION THAT BONYM WAS NOT THE HOLDER OF THE NOTE. ADDITIONALLY, THE FAC DOES NOT OVERCOME ZBS LAW'S STATUTORY PRIVILEGE BECAUSE IT DOES NOT ALLEGE FACTS THAT ZBS LAW LLP ACTED WITH MALICE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF TO ADDRESS IN GOOD FAITH ALL OF DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENTS MADE ON DEMURRER, NAMELY, HOW OR WHY A SCRIVENER'S ERROR WOULD RENDER THE INSTRUMENT VOID WHEN THE PROPERTY ADDRESS AND ASSESSOR'S PARCEL NUMBER ARE CORRECT; ADDRESSING THAT CALIFORNIA LAW DOES NOT REQUIRE ASSIGNMENTS OF THE DEED OF TRUST TO BE RECORDED; AND FACTS DEMONSTRATING WHY BONY AS HOLDER OF THE NOTE WOULD NOT BE PERMITTED TO ENFORCE THE TERMS OF THE DEED OF TRUST AS THE HOLDER OF THE NOTE ENDORSED IN BLANK (ATTACHED TO THE SAC). THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT THERE ARE NO ALLEGATIONS OF BAD FAITH AS TO SHELLPOINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED without leave to amend.
The allegations alleged in the Second Amended Complaint taken together with the judicially noticed documents do not show ZBS Law LLP's malice sufficient to overcome ZBS' statutory privilege under Civil Code sections 2924 and 47. Under subdivision (b) of Civil Code section 2924, the foreclosure trustee is protected from liability for good faith errors when it relies on information provided in good faith by the beneficiary regarding the nature and amount of the default under the secured obligation, deed of trust, or mortgage. The Court takes judicial notice of the (i) Deed of Trust, (ii) the declaration of Robert Orgeta, Custodian of Records for Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing which is the loan servicer for Bank of New York Mellon; (iii) the Substitution of Trustee; (iv), the Notice of Default, (v) the Notice of Trustee's Sale, (iv) the Trustee's Deed Upon Sale. With respect to the declaration, the Court is not taking judicial notice for the truth asserted therein, such as note ownership, beneficiary status, or authority to foreclose. Rather, they are being used to negate Plaintiff's claim of malice, namely, that ZBS had a reasonable belief in BONY's claim to ownership of the note and deed of trust, and in turn, their authority to foreclose. Plaintiff alleges that ZBS knew or recklessly disregarded that [BONY] was not the beneficiary of record and had not complied with 2924.17. (See, SAC paras. 34, 37.) But the declaration includes exhibits such as the Countrywide endorsed Note in blank and the Sale and Servicing Agreement dated June 1, 2007, evidencing the purchase of the Note and the rights under the Deed of Trust from Countrywide to BONY. Moreover, Civil Code 2924.17 does not require that an Assignment of a Deed of Trust be recorded as a prerequisite for a beneficiary to substitute a new trustee or for a trustee to have standing and authority to initiate nonjudicial foreclosure proceedings. The Court's conclusion that the judicially noticed documents provided ZBS a reasonable basis to believe in the legitimacy of the nonjudicial foreclosure proceeding and the truth of the documents ZBS recorded, is not a conclusion that their belief was accurate. (See, Schep v. Capital One, N.A. (2017) 12 Cal.App.5th 1331, 1337-1338.) Rather, it negates Plaintiff's claims of malice or reckless disregard as to the foreclosing trustee.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-02-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT NEWREZ LLC D.B.A. SHELLPOINT MORTGAGE SERVICING, THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED with leave to amend as to all causes of action for Plaintiff to address in good faith all of Defendants' arguments made on demurrer, namely, how or why a scrivener's error would render the instrument void when the property address and assessor's parcel number are correct; addressing that California law does not require assignments of the deed of trust to be recorded; and facts demonstrating why BONY as holder of the Note would not be permitted to enforce the terms of the Deed of Trust as the holder of the Note endorsed in blank (attached to the SAC). The Court also notes that there are no allegations of bad faith as to Shellpoint.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as to ZBS Law LLP for all causes action, with leave to amend to allege facts showing defects in the Deed and ZBS Law LLP's malice. The dismissal of BONYM's prior lawsuit did not constitute a judicial determination that BONYM was not the holder of the Note. Additionally, the FAC does not overcome ZBS Law's statutory privilege because it does not allege facts that ZBS Law LLP acted with malice.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND OBJECTION TO DECLARATION OF NONMONETARY STATUS BY FILED BY ZBS LAW, LLP.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's Motion to Strike Amended Complaint and Objection to Declaration of Non-Monetary Status is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that a motion to strike is a proper mechanism to address:
(1) "inherently deficient" causes of action whereas Plaintiff "cannot establish the necessary elements" of the claims because "the first amended complaint is premised on Plaintiffs' unsupported and erroneous conclusion that the foreclosing beneficiary, which holds the original wet-ink promissory note, lacks standing to foreclose;" or
(2) objections to the declaration of non-monetary status because the facts giving rise to Plaintiff's Complaint led the Plaintiff to allegedly erroneous conclusions. CC 2924l(c) requires Plaintiff to set forth facts, which Plaintiff did, however erroneous the moving defendant believes that Plaintiff's legal conclusions thereon may be. Moving party heavily relies on Kachlon v. Markowitz (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 316, a case decided at trial. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
